edges.description: ALMS1 loss compromises adipose tissue buffering capacity and promotes metabolically adverse obesity.
Cilia-linked signaling defects alter matrix remodeling programs and favor multiorgan fibrotic disease.
Renal tubular primary cilia lose normal maintenance and mechanosensory function.
Photoreceptor cells progressively fail when ALMS1-dependent ciliary trafficking is lost.
Hair-cell basal body dysfunction perturbs inner-ear sensory-cell polarity and survival.
Limited adipose buffering capacity accelerates systemic insulin resistance.
Progressive insulin resistance drives diabetes as beta-cell compensation fails.
Adipose tissue failure is accompanied by dyslipidemia with elevated triglycerides.
Matrix dysregulation accompanies common steatotic liver involvement in Alstrom syndrome.
Abnormal matrix remodeling contributes to infantile fibroelastosis and later adult cardiomyopathy.
Primary renal involvement progresses with age to chronic kidney disease.
Progressive cone and rod loss produces the hallmark retinal dystrophy.
Early photoreceptor loss causes severe lifelong visual disability.
Progressive predominantly sensory hearing loss reflects outer hair cell dysfunction and loss.
Infantile and adult myocardial remodeling commonly manifests as cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of ALMS1, a large gene with a tandem repeat encoding 47 amino acids, causes Alström syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We have detected six different mutations (two nonsense and four frameshift mutations causing premature stop codons) in seven families, confirming that ALMS1 is the gene underlying Alström syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Alström syndrome: a cross-sectional and follow-up study of 127 patients in China, highlighting genetic variant spectrum and cardiac features.
evidence.snippet: A total of 132 distinct ALMS1 variants (254 alleles) were identified, including 64 novel variants. Truncating mutations predominated (nonsense: 46.9%, frameshift: 45.3%).

nodes.description: ALMS1 is a centrosome and ciliary basal body-associated protein. Loss of ALMS1 function disrupts basal-body dependent cellular organization across multiple tissues and acts as the upstream lesion for the sensory, renal, metabolic, and fibrotic components of Alstrom syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Subcellular localization of ALMS1 supports involvement of centrosome and basal body dysfunction in the pathogenesis of obesity, insulin resistance, and type 2 diabetes.
evidence.snippet: We show that ALMS1 is widely expressed and localizes to centrosomes and to the base of cilia.

evidence.reference_title: Mutation of ALMS1, a large gene with a tandem repeat encoding 47 amino acids, causes Alström syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We have detected six different mutations (two nonsense and four frameshift mutations causing premature stop codons) in seven families, confirming that ALMS1 is the gene underlying Alström syndrome.

nodes.description: Cardiac disease in Alstrom syndrome spans infantile fibroelastotic cardiomyopathy and later adult myocardial remodeling, with both cardiomyocyte and cardiac fibroblast abnormalities implicated.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive ciliopathy mutations in primary endocardial fibroelastosis: a rare neonatal cardiomyopathy in a case of Alstrom syndrome.
evidence.snippet: In this case, the cardiac manifestation in Alstrom syndrome is pEFE.

evidence.reference_title: Recessive ciliopathy mutations in primary endocardial fibroelastosis: a rare neonatal cardiomyopathy in a case of Alstrom syndrome.
evidence.snippet: ALMS1-depleted cardiomyocytes exhibited enhanced proliferation activity.

evidence.reference_title: Defining the cardiovascular phenotype of adults with Alström syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Cardiomyopathy is common in adults with AS, complicated in a significant proportion by atherosclerotic coronary artery disease and restrictive cardiomyopathy, confirmed on CMR and invasive testing.

nodes.description: Age-progressive primary kidney involvement with declining kidney function and proteinuria in a subset of adults.

evidence.reference_title: Alström syndrome: Renal findings in correlation with obesity, insulin resistance, dyslipidemia and cardiomyopathy in 38 patients prospectively evaluated at the NIH clinical center.
evidence.snippet: Eighteen percent met the definition for chronic kidney disease (eGFR < 60 mL/min/1.73 m2 and proteinuria); all were adults with median age of 32.8 (20.6-37.9) years.

nodes.description: ALMS1 localizes to cochlear hair-cell basal bodies; its loss perturbs planar cell polarity and outer hair cell survival, producing progressive sensory hearing loss.

evidence.reference_title: Alström Syndrome protein ALMS1 localizes to basal bodies of cochlear hair cells and regulates cilium-dependent planar cell polarity.
evidence.snippet: These results identify previously unrecognized cochlear histopathologies associated with this ciliopathy that (i) implicate ALMS1 in planar cell polarity signaling and (ii) suggest that the loss of outer hair cells causes the majority of the hearing loss in Alström Syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Auditory and otologic profile of Alström syndrome: Comprehensive single center data on 38 patients.
evidence.snippet: Absent otoacoustic emissions, intact speech discrimination, and disproportionately normal auditory brainstem responses suggest an outer hair cell site of lesion.

nodes.description: Cochlear implantation should be considered for severe or functionally limiting hearing loss not adequately managed with amplification.

evidence.reference_title: Auditory and otologic profile of Alström syndrome: Comprehensive single center data on 38 patients.
evidence.snippet: These findings indicate that individuals with AS would benefit from sound amplification and if necessary, cochlear implantation.

nodes.description: Early progressive retinal degeneration involving cones and rods, often beginning in infancy and driving lifelong visual disability.

evidence.reference_title: Alström syndrome: an ultra-rare monogenic disorder as a model for insulin resistance, type 2 diabetes mellitus and obesity.
evidence.snippet: The infantile cone-rod dystrophy with nystagmus and severe visual impairment is the earliest and most consistent clinical manifestation of ALMS.

nodes.description: Infantile or later-onset myocardial disease with ventricular dilation and systolic dysfunction; infantile episodes may partially recover while adult cardiomyopathy can progress.

evidence.reference_title: Spectrum of ALMS1 variants and evaluation of genotype-phenotype correlations in Alström syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Alström syndrome is a monogenic recessive disorder featuring an array of clinical manifestations, with systemic fibrosis and multiple organ involvement, including retinal degeneration, hearing loss, childhood obesity, diabetes mellitus, dilated cardiomyopathy (DCM), urological dysfunction, and pulmonary, hepatic, and renal failure.

evidence.reference_title: Defining the cardiovascular phenotype of adults with Alström syndrome.
evidence.snippet: RESULTS: 47 adults with AS (64% male; mean age 33 years; 66% white British) were studied. Seven (15%) survived infantile cardiomyopathy and 23 (49%) developed adult-onset cardiomyopathy.

nodes.description: ALMS1 deficiency alters extracellular matrix regulatory programs, favoring collagen remodeling and fibroproliferative disease in liver and heart.

evidence.reference_title: Loss of the centrosomal protein ALMS1 alters lipid metabolism and the regulation of extracellular matrix-related processes.
evidence.snippet: alterations associated with ALMS1 depletion clustered around the processes of extracellular matrix regulation and lipid metabolism in both the transcriptome and proteome.

nodes.description: Semaglutide or exenatide can improve weight, glycemia, and lipid parameters in adults with Alstrom syndrome and metabolically significant obesity or diabetes.

evidence.reference_title: Glucagon-like peptide-1 analogues in monogenic syndromic obesity: Real-world data from a large cohort of Alström syndrome patients.
evidence.snippet: equating to 6% weight loss (P < .01) and 1.1% absolute reduction in HbA1c (P < .01).

nodes.description: Sound amplification is beneficial for the common progressive sensory hearing loss of Alstrom syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Auditory and otologic profile of Alström syndrome: Comprehensive single center data on 38 patients.
evidence.snippet: These findings indicate that individuals with AS would benefit from sound amplification and if necessary, cochlear implantation.

nodes.description: Common metabolic liver involvement that often coexists with early fibrogenic liver stiffness abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Alström syndrome: a cross-sectional and follow-up study of 127 patients in China, highlighting genetic variant spectrum and cardiac features.
evidence.snippet: Clinical manifestations included universal visual impairment (100%, 112/112), obesity (84.1%, 96/114), hearing loss (70.8%, 51/72), hepatic steatosis (66.7%, 40/60), and cardiac abnormalities (58.2%, 46/79).

nodes.description: Early and often severe insulin resistance complicating obesity in Alstrom syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Relative Adipose Tissue Failure in Alström Syndrome Drives Obesity-Induced Insulin Resistance.
evidence.snippet: Here, we report on a monogenic form of IR-prone obesity, Alström syndrome (ALMS).

nodes.description: Renal disease in Alstrom syndrome reflects a primary nephron ciliopathy rather than merely secondary metabolic injury, with progressive tubular ciliary loss and impaired mechanosensory signaling.

evidence.reference_title: A role for Alström syndrome protein, alms1, in kidney ciliogenesis and cellular quiescence.
evidence.snippet: Here, we show that in vitro knockdown of Alms1 in mice causes stunted cilia on kidney epithelial cells and prevents these cells from increasing calcium influx in response to mechanical stimuli.

evidence.reference_title: Alström syndrome: Renal findings in correlation with obesity, insulin resistance, dyslipidemia and cardiomyopathy in 38 patients prospectively evaluated at the NIH clinical center.
evidence.snippet: After adjusting for age, there were no significant associations of kidney dysfunction with type 2 diabetes mellitus, dyslipidemia, hypertension, cardiomyopathy or portal hypertension suggesting that kidney disease in AS is a primary manifestation of the syndrome due to lack of ALMS1 protein.

nodes.description: Early-onset obesity, often beginning in childhood and frequently truncal in distribution.

evidence.reference_title: Alström syndrome: a cross-sectional and follow-up study of 127 patients in China, highlighting genetic variant spectrum and cardiac features.
evidence.snippet: Clinical manifestations included universal visual impairment (100%, 112/112), obesity (84.1%, 96/114), hearing loss (70.8%, 51/72), hepatic steatosis (66.7%, 40/60), and cardiac abnormalities (58.2%, 46/79).

nodes.description: ALMS1 deficiency disrupts photoreceptor intracellular trafficking and drives progressive cone and rod degeneration, producing the characteristic retinal dystrophy of Alstrom syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Alms1-disrupted mice recapitulate human Alström syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Diminished cone ERG b-wave response is observed early, followed by the degeneration of photoreceptor cells.

evidence.reference_title: Alström syndrome: a cross-sectional and follow-up study of 127 patients in China, highlighting genetic variant spectrum and cardiac features.
evidence.snippet: BACKGROUND: Alström syndrome (ALMS) is a rare autosomal recessive multisystem disorder caused by biallelic pathogenic variants in the ALMS1 gene, characterized by progressive cone-rod dystrophy, early-onset obesity, cardiomyopathy, and multiorgan dysfunction.

nodes.description: Obesity in Alstrom syndrome is accompanied by inadequate adipose tissue expansion and adipocyte dysfunction, producing a metabolically fragile state in which insulin resistance develops early and progresses rapidly.

evidence.reference_title: Relative Adipose Tissue Failure in Alström Syndrome Drives Obesity-Induced Insulin Resistance.
evidence.snippet: Hence, we show for the first time the relative AT failure in human obese cohorts to be a major determinant of accelerated IR without evidence of lipodystrophy.

nodes.description: Progressive, usually symmetric sensory hearing loss beginning in childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Auditory and otologic profile of Alström syndrome: Comprehensive single center data on 38 patients.
evidence.snippet: Our data show that hearing loss associated with AS begins in childhood and is a predominantly symmetric, sensory hearing loss that may progress to a severe degree.

nodes.description: Progressive hyperglycemia emerging as insulin resistance worsens over time.

evidence.reference_title: Alström syndrome: an ultra-rare monogenic disorder as a model for insulin resistance, type 2 diabetes mellitus and obesity.
evidence.snippet: In addition, infantile transient cardiomyopathy, early childhood obesity with hyperphagia, deafness, insulin resistance (IR), type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM), systemic fibrosis and progressive renal or liver dysfunction are common findings.

nodes.description: Severe early-onset visual loss caused by progressive retinal degeneration.

evidence.reference_title: Alström syndrome: a cross-sectional and follow-up study of 127 patients in China, highlighting genetic variant spectrum and cardiac features.
evidence.snippet: Clinical manifestations included universal visual impairment (100%, 112/112), obesity (84.1%, 96/114), hearing loss (70.8%, 51/72), hepatic steatosis (66.7%, 40/60), and cardiac abnormalities (58.2%, 46/79).
